_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for pages/support/grading-info.html. Edit values here, then run render_grading_info.py.
_meta.schemaNote: One outer lc-card with internal lc-named-section blocks (home-page pattern, not 4-cards-stacked).

gradeWeights.label: Grade Weights
gradeWeights.footer_html: Master Labs are your module capstone — the first assignment that uses concepts from BOTH chapters together.

dropPolicy.label: Sick Days
dropPolicy.intro_html: Life happens. Each category has one sick day built in — the lowest grade in each category is dropped automatically.

callout.title: How sick days work
callout.body_html: Just like at work, sick days aren’t unlimited. Take more than one day at work and it comes out of your check in the form of forfeited wages. Take more than one drop here and it comes out of your GPA in the form of forfeited grades. Plan around them — don’t spend them early.

lateWork.label: Late Work

callout.title: No Late Work. No Exceptions.
callout.body_html: Whatever the deadline is, that’s the deadline. No exceptions, no extensions, no “I just need one more day.” Don’t ask — the answer is no, and asking only makes both of us feel bad.

sections.heading: Why it works this way
sections.body_html:
This isn’t academic strictness — it’s workplace simulation. In the field, deadlines don’t slide. A software release ships on the planned date or the client launches without it. A sprint ends Friday whether your feature is done or not. A bug fix gets to production before the audit, or the team explains in writing why it didn’t. The professional standard you’ll be measured by once you graduate is binary: did you deliver, or didn’t you?

Think about it from the other side. Does walking into your boss’s office without the work product you were supposed to deliver sound like something you’ll get away with? Maybe once. Twice? Pack your box, you’re done. The companies you’ll work for don’t have a “lowest grade dropped” policy.

I hold you to that same standard here on purpose. If I slid deadlines for “I forgot” or “I had a busy week,” you’d graduate without the muscle memory to meet deadlines that actually matter — and your first job would be where you learned what hard deadlines feel like. That’s not a favor I’m willing to do you. Treat this course like a job, and you’ll do great.

sections.heading: How to plan around it
sections.body_html: Submit to CodeGrade early and often before the deadline. Most lab issues surface in the first few submissions, and you have unlimited submissions until the cutoff — use them. Don’t save the work for the last hour. If something genuinely catastrophic happens, your sick days are designed for exactly that.

sections.heading: End of term
sections.body_html: The final cutoff for the semester is 11:59 PM Tuesday, August 4. Canvas locks at that point — there’s no path to submit anything after the term closes. Plan your final week to wrap everything up before then.

lateWork.closing_html: Learn to work this way now, while the consequence is a grade. Once you’re employed, the consequence is your reputation, your contract, or your job.
